untranslatable_reason: Signal requires multi-timeframe analysis framework (lower/intermediate/macro timeframe hierarchy with alignment validation, contradiction detection across timeframes, and support/resistance/pattern/momentum recognition). The backtest engine schema only supports single-timeframe indicator conditions. The core logic—'validate lower timeframe setup against higher timeframe structure and macro bias'—cannot be expressed as atomic conditions on allowed indicators. This requires: (1) timeframe parameter specification, (2) cross-timeframe comparison operators, (3) pattern/structure recognition beyond price/indicator thresholds, and (4) contradiction/alignment scoring logic. Provided fallback uses trend alignment proxy via moving average crossovers, but loses the essential multi-timeframe filter mechanism.
translation_notes: GRIMES_DRY_7_0 is a meta-rule about trade validation methodology, not a mechanical trading rule. The signal describes a decision framework (use HTF to filter/confirm LTF setups) rather than specific entry/exit triggers. Fallback conditions approximate trend-following with MA alignment but cannot capture: (1) the prohibition of trades contradicted by any HTF level, (2) the requirement for positive HTF confirmation, (3) the distinction between invalidation (contradictions) vs. validation (alignment). Recommend implementing this as a separate filter module in backtest engine rather than as a condition-based rule.
